operation: First, create a 2D sketch: draw an inner arc centered at (0,0) with radius 80 spanning from +20 degrees to -20 degrees, draw a straight radial segment connecting the +20 degree end of the inner arc to the +20 degree point on the outer radius, draw an outer arc centered at (0,0) with radius 100 spanning from +20 degrees to -20 degrees, then close the sketch by connecting the -20 degree end of the outer arc back to the -20 degree end of the inner arc to form a 40-degree annular sector profile. Place this sketch on the XY workplane, then extrude the profile 340 units along the Z-axis to generate the final 3D solid.
geometry: This is an elongated straight prism with a narrow annular sector cross-section. It has two concentric curved long faces (one inner, one outer) running its entire length, connected by two flat radial side faces, plus two flat parallel end faces that are perpendicular to the part's long axis.
function: This part is a structural segment for curved annular assemblies, such as sections of gas turbine frames, curved support rails, or segmented cylindrical housings, designed to provide rigid support while fitting into curved assembly layouts.
